ranked_tasks.reasoning: This task carries a critical risk level with 2 rollovers indicating persistent blockage, and directly blocks 3 downstream dependencies on the NGIS Phase 3 project. The vendor delay (risk_001) has already been escalated to the Director and is the root cause of cascading schedule risk across proj_001, making resolution the single highest-leverage action available. Combined with the March 10 deadline and maximum stakeholder priority (5), this task demands immediate attention.

This task addresses a formal ITSG-33 compliance finding (risk_002) raised by TBS, with a March 12 deadline and zero rollovers — meaning it has not yet been started despite the compliance finding email dated March 5. Failure to submit SA&A documentation to the TBS audit office carries regulatory and reputational consequences for the department, and the task blocks 2 additional dependencies. Stakeholder priority is at maximum (5) and the risk level is critical.

A $340,000 (14%) budget overrun (risk_003) requires Director approval by March 10, and this task has already rolled over once, indicating prior delay in securing sign-off. The task blocks 1 dependency and carries high risk; without budget revision approval, proj_002 activities may be halted or face compliance issues. Stakeholder priority of 4 and the imminent deadline reinforce urgency.

This task has the earliest deadline of all tasks (March 7) and is currently not started, creating an immediate urgency gap. Although it blocks no dependencies directly, the impact assessment of the vendor delay on Site A migration cutover is a prerequisite for informed decision-making on task_001 and broader NGIS Phase 3 scheduling. Without this analysis, the team is managing the vendor delay (rank 1 task) without a clear picture of downstream cutover consequences.

The potential departure of the sole AD migration expert (risk_004) represents a critical single point of failure for proj_001, and this task blocks the highest number of dependencies (4) of any task in the list. Although the March 17 deadline provides slightly more runway than the March 10 cluster, the not_started status and the irreversible nature of knowledge loss if the expert departs before transfer is complete make this a high-urgency item. This task is in progress with a March 10 deadline and blocks 1 dependency, placing it in the same deadline cluster as tasks 001, 003. However, its medium risk level and low stakeholder priority (2) position it below the higher-stakes items. Completion is needed to keep UAT on schedule for proj_001, and the in-progress status suggests it is closer to completion than not-started peers at the same deadline.

With 3 rollovers, this is the most chronically deferred task in the list, suggesting systemic blockers or deprioritization. Although it has no hard deadline and blocks no dependencies, it is directly relevant to resolving risk_001 (vendor delay) and could provide contingency options if the primary vendor cannot deliver. The rollover pattern warrants a deliberate decision: either resource it properly or formally defer it with documented rationale.

The Treasury Board submission for GC-Cloud Wave 4 has a March 15 deadline and is not yet started, but carries medium risk with no dependencies blocked and a stakeholder priority of 2. TB submissions have fixed external deadlines and missing them can delay cloud adoption approvals, so timely initiation is warranted. No rollovers and no active risk record reduce urgency relative to higher-ranked items.

This task has a low risk level, no dependencies blocked, and a deadline of April 1 — providing adequate runway relative to the current March priority cluster. It is not started but is a standard communications deliverable with no active risk record attached. It should be scheduled for initiation once the March 10–17 critical cluster is resolved.

This is a purely administrative formatting task with no risk level assigned, zero stakeholder priority, no dependencies blocked, and an April 15 deadline. It has no associated active risks and no strategic consequence if deferred within its deadline window. It should be completed as a low-effort housekeeping item when bandwidth permits.
